expectations.text: Recommends `winml inspect` (or equivalent inspection step) before any build/benchmark work.
expectations.evidence: Inspect step explicitly included as a golden-rule preflight.

expectations.text: Identifies QNN as the EP for Snapdragon X Elite NPU.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'Snapdragon X Elite ... QNN execution provider ...'

expectations.text: Recommends building the model once and then running `winml perf` twice -- once on NPU, once on CPU -- rather than building two separate pipelines.
expectations.evidence: Response explains 'one config, one build that produces both artifacts, two perf runs.'

expectations.text: Specifies that the CPU run should use the optimized (pre-compile) artifact, NOT the QNN-compiled artifact.
expectations.evidence: Quote: 'CPU run: optimized (pre-compile) ONNX, on the CPU EP. Same source model, same optimization passes ...'

expectations.text: Warns that EP-compiled artifacts are EP-locked / can't be run on a different EP and produce nonsense numbers.
expectations.evidence: Quote: EP-compiled artifacts are bound to the EP they were compiled for; trying CPU EP with a QNN-compiled .onnx is at best meaningless.

expectations.text: Either directs user to `winml --help`, OR uses only common/plausible flag patterns.
expectations.evidence: All flags real; references `winml <cmd> --help` for flag verification.

expectations.text: Includes an install/prereq block (or a clear pointer back to install steps) -- the user's prompt does not signal prior winml usage, so the default-include-install rule applies.
expectations.evidence: Prereq install block included with uv venv + AITK wheel + verify.

expectations.text: All `winml` commands quoted in the response are runnable -- every flag exists in `winml <cmd> --help`, no positional model arg where the CLI is flag-only.
expectations.evidence: All ? winml commands verified runnable against --help.
